title: Factors that affect a credit score
description: Practice understanding of hat lowers, increases, or does not affect a credit score
topic_path: Factors that affect a credit score

assessment_items.question_content:
You recently paid off one of your credit cards.

**What will happen to your credit score?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Credit score will go up.
Credit score will go down.
Credit score will not change.

assessment_items.question_content:
You recently lowered your total amount of debt owed by paying off some bank loans.

**How will this affect your credit score?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Your credit score will gain points.
Your credit score will lose points.
There will be no change in your credit score.

assessment_items.question_content:
You recently were able to get an error corrected on your credit report. Before it was corrected, the error showed you had defaulted on a bill.

**What will happen to your credit score after this  correction?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Your credit score will stay the same.
Your credit score will be negatively impacted.
Your credit score will be positively impacted.

assessment_items.question_content:
Last month you forgot to pay your credit card bill. This month you made two payments in order to catch up.

**How will this affect your credit score?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It will increase the credit score.
It will decrease the credit score.
The credit score will not change.

assessment_items.question_content:
You apply for every credit card that is offered to you. This gets you discounts when shopping.

**How will your desire to get discounts by applying for credit cards affect your credit score?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It will lower the credit score.
It will raise the credit score.
The credit score will not change.

assessment_items.question_content:
Last year you filed for bankruptcy. This was the result of not being able to pay your bills.

**Based on this bankruptcy, how will your credit score be impacted?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Your credit score will be negatively impacted.
Your credit score will be positively impacted.
There will be no impact on your credit score.

assessment_items.question_content:
You recently got a promotion and a pay raise at work.

**What effect will a pay raise have on your credit score?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: increase of the credit score
decrease of the credit score
no change to credit score

assessment_items.question_content:
You recently inherited a large sum of money. 

**Based on this money, what will happen to your credit score?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It will have no effect on your credit score.
Your credit score will rise.
Your credit score will fall.

assessment_items.question_content:
Ida just paid her friend back the cash that she owed her. 

**How is Ida's repayment of the loan shown on a credit score?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Her credit score will go down.
Her credit score will rise.
Her credit score will stay the same.

assessment_items.question_content:
Credit inquiries are checks on your credit report. They can be either soft (not affecting your score) or hard (potentially lowering your score).

**Label each action as either a soft or hard inquiry.**

[[☃ categorizer 1]]
hints: To determine a hard inquiry from a soft inquiry think if the inquiry is related to a *lending* decision or a *background check*.

**Hard inquiries** happen when a financial institution checks your credit report as part of a *lending decision*, typically when you apply for a loan, credit card, or mortgage. 

[[☃ explanation 1]]

**Soft inquiries** happen when a person or company checks your credit report as part of a *background check*, like when you are applying for an apartment, or just checking your own report.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

items: applying for a store credit card

assessment_items.question_content:
Credit inquiries are checks on your credit report. They can be either soft (not affecting your score) or hard (potentially lowering your score).

**Label each action as either a soft or hard inquiry.**

[[☃ categorizer 1]]
hints: To determine a hard inquiry from a soft inquiry think if the inquiry is related to a *lending* decision or a *background check*.

**Hard inquiries** happen when a financial institution checks your credit report as part of a *lending decision*, typically when *you* apply for a mortgage or car loan. 

[[☃ explanation 1]]

**Soft inquiries** happen when a person or company checks your credit report as part of a *background check.*  They then decide whether or not to send you pre-approved credit card offers in the mail. 

[[☃ explanation 1]]

items: pre-approved credit offers in the mail

assessment_items.question_content:
Credit inquiries are checks on your credit report. They can be either soft (not affecting your score) or hard (potentially lowering your score).

**Label each action as either a soft or hard inquiry.**

[[☃ categorizer 1]]
hints: To determine a hard inquiry from a soft inquiry think if the inquiry is related to a *lending* decision or a *background check*.

**Hard inquiries** happen when a financial institution checks your credit report as part of a *lending decision*, One example of this is when you try to qualify for a phone installment plan. 

[[☃ explanation 1]]

**Soft inquiries** happen when a person or company checks your credit report as part of a *background check*, like when you are subject to an employee background check for a job or when insurance companies are pre-qualifying your for their products. 
[[☃ explanation 1]]

items: qualifying for a phone installment payment plan
